name: NICHQVanderbiltTeacherInitial
title: NICHQ Vanderbilt Assessment Scale — Teacher Informant (Initial)
copyright: © 2002 American Academy of Pediatrics and National Initiative for Children's Healthcare Quality. Adapted from the Vanderbilt Rating Scales developed by Mark L. Wolraich, MD.

code.display: NICHQ Vanderbilt Assessment Scale — Teacher informant [NICHQ]

item.text: Rate each item based on the child's behavior over the past 6 months.
Fails to give attention to details or makes careless mistakes in schoolwork
Has difficulty sustaining attention to tasks or activities
Does not seem to listen when spoken to directly
Does not follow through on instructions and fails to finish schoolwork (not due to oppositional behavior or failure to understand)
Has difficulty organizing tasks and activities
Avoids, dislikes, or is reluctant to engage in tasks that require sustained mental effort
Loses things necessary for tasks or activities (school assignments, pencils, or books)
Is easily distracted by extraneous stimuli
Fidgets with hands or feet or squirms in seat
Leaves seat in classroom or in other situations in which remaining seated is expected
Runs about or climbs excessively in situations in which remaining seated is expected
Has difficulty playing or engaging in leisure activities quietly
Is "on the go" or often acts as if "driven by a motor"
Blurts out answers before questions have been completed
Interrupts or intrudes on others (e.g., butts into conversations/games)
Actively defies or refuses to comply with adult's requests or rules
Lies to obtain goods for favors or to avoid obligations (e.g., "cons" others)
Has stolen items of nontrivial value
Is afraid to try new things for fear of making mistakes
Blames self for problems, feels guilty
Feels lonely, unwanted, or unloved; complains that "no one loves him/her"
Inattention — count rated Often / Very Often (DSM threshold: ≥6)
Hyperactivity/Impulsivity — count rated Often / Very Often (≥6)
